stories.headline: Israel–Lebanon truce begins under strict watch and early violations
stories.body: A 10-day ceasefire brokered by Washington between Israel and Lebanon took effect just after midnight, sending displaced families cautiously toward home even as Israeli troops kept a 10km-deep security zone, Trump invited both leaders to the White House and the Lebanese army reported fresh Israeli shelling minutes into the truce.

stories.headline: Australian war hero Ben Roberts-Smith granted bail on murder charges
stories.body: Judge Greg Grogan released Ben Roberts-Smith on A$250,000 surety, ordering thrice-weekly police check-ins, device access and a surrendered passport after defence lawyers argued the unprecedented Afghan war crimes case could take years before trial; prosecutors allege he killed or ordered the killing of unarmed detainees between 2009 and 2012, allegations he denies.

stories.headline: South Africa’s Julius Malema sentenced to five years for firing rifle at rally
stories.body: Magistrate Twanet Olivier handed Economic Freedom Fighters leader Julius Malema a five-year prison term for illegally firing a semi-automatic rifle during the party’s 2018 anniversary, stressing public officials’ duty to avoid glorifying gun violence; Malema remains free while appealing and vowed to fight what he called politically motivated justice.

stories.headline: Naples bank hostages freed as robbers escape through tunnel
stories.body: Italian police freed 25 staff and customers held at gunpoint in a Crédit Agricole branch in Naples after a two-hour standoff, but the three masked robbers vanished through a hole in the floor into the sewer network, apparently targeting safety deposit boxes and leaving shaken hostages without serious injuries.

stories.headline: ICE acting director Todd Lyons to step down amid scrutiny of Trump-era crackdowns
stories.body: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin said acting ICE chief Todd Lyons will leave at the end of May for the private sector after shepherding mass deportation operations that drew condemnation over fatal arrests in Minneapolis and California even as the Trump administration praised his aggressive expansion of the agency.

stories.headline: Singer D4vd arrested in Los Angeles murder case tied to teen’s remains
stories.body: Los Angeles police arrested TikTok star David Anthony Burke, known as D4vd, on suspicion of murdering 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez after her dismembered body was found in his Tesla last September; he is being held without bail while prosecutors review grand jury evidence, and his lawyers insist he did not kill her.

stories.headline: France presses US to free 85-year-old widow detained by ICE
stories.body: The French consulate confirmed it is lobbying the Department of Homeland Security to release Marie-Thérèse Ross, an 85-year-old widow of a US Army veteran who was arrested in Alabama on 1 April for overstaying a visa and moved to a Louisiana detention center despite frail health, becoming a flashpoint in Trump’s deportation drive against military families.

stories.headline: Sánchez and Lula convene first Spain–Brazil summit to rally progressive bloc
stories.body: Pedro Sánchez and Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva opened an unprecedented Spain–Brazil summit in Barcelona with ten ministers from each government, framing their joint agenda on multilateralism, climate action, Mercosur and digital regulation as a counterweight to Trump-era unilateralism ahead of a weekend of progressive fora in the city.

stories.headline: PP and Vox seal Extremadura coalition with hard-line immigration agenda
stories.body: After months of stalemate, María Guardiola and Vox agreed to revive their Extremadura coalition, giving the far-right party a vice-presidency, two ministries and a senate seat in exchange for supporting budgets while enacting 74 measures that prioritize Spaniards for housing, curb aid to irregular migrants and seek to repatriate unaccompanied minors.

stories.headline: UCO report shows Armengol leaned on Koldo for pandemic procurement
stories.body: A 195-page Guardia Civil report on the Koldo mask case details WhatsApp exchanges where then Balearic president Francina Armengol asked aide Koldo García to reach minister José Luis Ábalos about PCR testing and to source children’s masks, bolstering investigators’ claims he brokered access for business interests while her office insists technicians made purchasing decisions.

stories.headline: Regularisation drive triggers five-hour queues at Barcelona help centres
stories.body: Barcelona’s municipal offices saw dawn lines stretching for hours as undocumented residents sought vulnerability reports, padró updates and other paperwork on the first day of Spain’s mass regularisation, prompting the city to reinforce services while Catalan NGOs warn of digital barriers that push applicants toward in-person appointments before the 30 June deadline.

stories.headline: Merchants pitch Pelai Street makeover as city plans post-2027 revamp
stories.body: Retail alliance Barcelona Oberta unveiled an architect’s proposal to turn Carrer Pelai into a greener single-lane boulevard with wider pavements and curated urban furniture, while the Collboni administration signalled the corridor’s full redesign will be folded into the next mandate’s mobility works linking Plaça Universitat to Passeig de Sant Joan.

stories.headline: Ombudsman sounds alarm over neglect at Raval’s historic Hospital gardens
stories.body: Barcelona’s Síndic de Greuges urged emergency maintenance of the Hospital de la Santa Creu gardens after inspections found broken paving, failing irrigation and encampments in the heritage site, pressing the city to pair social outreach with conservation so the emblematic Raval space can be safely reopened to neighbours.

stories.headline: Barcelona’s memory plan elevates La Model and Montjuïc as civic anchors
stories.body: The city approved a 2026-2030 Democratic Memory Plan that turns the former Model prison, Montjuïc castle and Fabra i Coats into flagship sites for exhibitions, archives and educational programs, aligning municipal investments with a global progressive summit weekend to position Barcelona as a hub for defending democratic values.
